question.text: Under Section 18A of SCRA, when are contracts in derivatives considered legal and valid?

answer.text: When traded on a recognized stock exchange and settled on the clearing house of such exchange as per its rules and bye-laws
When entered into between any two parties with a written agreement
When approved by SEBI on a case-by-case basis
When the contract value exceeds a minimum threshold set by the government

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Section 18A states that contracts in derivatives shall be legal and valid if they are (a) traded on a recognized stock exchange, and (b) settled on the clearing house of the recognized stock exchange, in accordance with the rules and bye-laws of such exchange. **The Trap:** OTC derivative contracts between parties without exchange involvement do not qualify under this section. The dual requirement of exchange trading AND clearing house settlement is mandatory. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Section 18A provides the legal framework for exchange-traded derivatives in India. This is why SEBI-regulated exchanges and clearing corporations are essential.
